title: Dopamine measurement by HPLC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"54mr0","text":"Here we describe the methods for dopamine measurement in midbrain organoids by HPLC.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"np2ae","text":"- 140 mM NaCl","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"iwwd4","text":"- 2.5 mM KCl","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a3pms","text":"- 1 mM MgCl2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fzkml","text":"- 1.8 mM CaCl2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fslar","text":"- 20 mM HEPES","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vg5o0","text":"- 56 mM KCL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tusj","text":"- 250 μM ascorbic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"x8twn","text":"- 0.05 mM Na2EDTA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hrfjy","text":"- 4 mM formic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ypjd","text":"- 0.1 M phosphate buffer (pH 3.0)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"i74uy","text":"- 0.1 mM EDTA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54wkg","text":"- 10% methanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: To measure dopamine levels in organoid lysates, high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) with electrochemical detection was employed. Organoids were incubated in physiological medium containing 140 mM NaCl; 2.5 mM KCl; 1 mM MgCl2; 1.8 mM CaCl2 and 20 mM HEPES at pH 7.4.

To measure the release of neurotransmitters under high KCL concentrations, organoids were incubated in high KCL medium (previous physiological medium with 56 mM KCL, the concentration of NaCl was proportionally reduced to keep the same osmolarity). Solutions were collected after 20 minutes of incubation.

The incubation solutions and the homogenates were centrifuged at 20,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C. Supernatants were collected for analysis and were mixed with stabilization medium resulting in a final concentration of 250 μM ascorbic acid, 0.05 mM Na2EDTA and 4 mM formic acid.

Dopamine content was measured using an HPLC system equipped with an electrochemical detector (Agilent Technologies). Separation was achieved on a C18 reversed-phase column with a mobile phase consisting of 0.1 M phosphate buffer (pH 3.0), 0.1 mM EDTA, and 10% methanol at a flow rate of 1.0 ml/min.
Dopamine was quantified by comparing peak areas with those of standard solutions and normalized according to the protein contents of individual organoids.
creator: Hariam Raji
